_meta.purpose: Manual overrides applied on top of LiteLLM's snapshot by tools/refresh-pricing.sh. Entries here win over upstream so manual fixes for newly-announced Google/Anthropic/OpenAI rates aren't blown away when LiteLLM hasn't caught up yet.
_meta.policy: Drop an entry from this file once LiteLLM publishes the same or better rates (i.e. once the upstream snapshot would produce the same numbers we have here). Add an `_until` ISO date if you want a temporary override that auto-expires.

gemini-3.5-flash._note: Antigravity 2 default model. Official pricing announced at Google I/O 2026 (2026-05-19): $1.50/M input, $9.00/M output, $0.15/M cached input (90% discount).

gemini-3.5-flash-thinking._note: Gemini 3.5 Flash's Extended thinking mode. Same per-token rate card as the base model — Google bills thinking tokens at the output rate, so a session with extended thinking enabled produces a larger output-token total but the price per token is unchanged.

gemini-3.1-pro._note: Antigravity 2 frontier Pro model, announced alongside 3.5 Flash at Google I/O 2026 (2026-05-19). Tiered pricing: $2.00/M input + $12.00/M output for prompts up to 200K tokens; $4.00/M + $18.00/M above 200K. Cache discount mirrors 3.5 Flash's 90% (Google's stated default for the 3.x line).

claude-opus-4-8._note: Claude Opus 4.8 (released 2026-05-28). $5/M input, $25/M output — unchanged from Opus 4.7 per Anthropic's launch; LiteLLM may lag the release by a few days, so this override guarantees the app prices it and matches ccusage. Sources: anthropic.com/claude/opus, platform.claude.com/docs/about-claude/pricing.

moonshotai/kimi-k2.5._note: OpenRouter catalog on 2026-06-06 lists MoonshotAI Kimi K2.5 at $0.40/M input, $1.90/M output, and $0.09/M cache read. Pricing aliases Cursor's composer-2.5 and composer-2.5-fast ids to this row so Cursor Composer usage contributes dollar cost while generic cursor-auto remains unpriced.
